summary: Althea shines, China Cat tight, high‑energy second set
blurb: The show delivers a standout Althea with sweet Garcia lead and a razor‑sharp China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider transition. Jerry’s tone stays otherworldly throughout, while Phil’s crystal‑clear bass underpins the jam‑heavy second half featuring Estimated Prophet into Eyes of the World. Brent’s keyboards sit perfectly in the mix and the dual drummers drive a powerful rhythm section.
review: On March 21, 1981 at London’s Rainbow Theatre the Dead were firing on all cylinders. The first set builds from an exuberant Alabama Getaway into a transcendent Althea where Garcia’s lead is described as “sweet” and otherworldly, then rolls straight into a tight Let It Grow > China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider sequence that keeps the momentum soaring. The second set kicks off with Jack Straw and moves through Ship Of Fools into an electrifying Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World stretch, highlighted by volcanic Black Peter solo work and a high‑octane Not Fade Away finish. Throughout, Phil’s bass shines crystal‑clear, Brent’s keyboards “kick out” on every song, and both Mickey and Billy lay down punchy, articulate drumming that drives the band forward. The performance is captured best on the Clugston soundboard tape – crisp, balanced, and free of drop‑outs – making this a must‑listen for anyone craving a quintessential early‑’80s Dead experience.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All six recordings are marked positive, with multiple reviewers praising the same standout moments (Althea, China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider, Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World) and consistently describing the band as locked‑in and high‑energy; no negative comments were noted.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard source with crisp, clear mix and full bass presence; captures the Althea lead and tight China Cat performance without drop‑outs.

key_highlights: Sweet, transcendent Althea lead and volcanic Black Peter solo
Tight China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider segue that drives the set
Soaring Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World jam with powerful rhythm section
Crystal‑clear bass and punchy dual drums keep energy high throughout

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers a sweet, transcendent lead on Althea, tight otherworldly tone on China Cat, web‑like solos throughout, and an aggressive, volcanic Black Peter solo that many call the best ever.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh’s bass is crystal‑clear with double‑ and triple‑stops that lock in the groove; his low end shines especially during the jam‑heavy second set.
band_performance.Bob: Weir’s slide guitar shows noticeable improvement, and although his vocal level spikes early in the second set, it settles quickly without detracting from the performance.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland’s keyboards sit perfectly in the mix, kicking out on every song; his solo on ‘Rooster’ (though not on the setlist) is praised as non‑pareil, underscoring his strong presence throughout the show.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums are described as great and punchy, driving the band forward with articulate fills that add power to the high‑energy jams.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann “trashed” his kit in a solid, consistent fashion, providing a sturdy backbone for the dual‑drum attack.
